items.title: Wheat eases from 2-year high but Black Sea supply woes limit downside
items.summary:
SINGAPORE: Chicago wheat slid on Thursday, as the market took a breather after climbing to a two-year high in the previous session, although the decline was limited by attacks on grain vessels and port infrastructure in the Black Sea region.
Chicago wheat jumps on renewed attacks on Black Sea ports
Soybeans firmed, tracking higher crude prices, while corn ticked lower.

items.title: Pakistan’s business confidence improves in Q2, but budget concerns persist, finds survey
items.summary:
Business confidence in Pakistan recovered during the second quarter of 2026, with companies reporting improved current conditions and stronger expectations for the year ahead, according to the latest Business Confidence Index released by Gallup Pakistan on Thursday.
The survey, conducted from July 2 to 7 among 527 businesses across manufacturing, services, construction, financial services, and who

items.title: Australian shares hit near five-week high; Macquarie CEO Wikramanayake to retire
items.summary:
Australian shares rose to a near five-week high on Thursday, with mining and real estate stocks leading gains ahead of jobs data, while investment bank Macquarie Group inched higher after announcing the retirement of its longtime CEO.
The S&P/ASX 200 index climbed 1.1% to 8,918.8 by 0115 GMT, hitting its highest level since June 18.
The benchmark gained 0.3% on Wednesday. Investors were awaiti

items.title: Houthis say they attacked Saudi tankers in the Red Sea, threatening new chokepoint in Iran war
items.summary:
CAIRO: The Iranian-aligned Houthis said on Thursday they struck two Saudi oil tankers as part of a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, threatening to create a ​second chokepoint on global oil supplies alongside Iran’s near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
Meanwhile the US military completed a new round of strikes on Iran at President Donald Trump’s direction, marking a 12th ‌successive night of Americ

items.title: India bonds poised for another weak opening with oil, rupee in focus
items.summary:
MUMBAI: Indian government bonds may open lower again on Thursday as crude oil prices rise towards $100 per barrel because of the worsening Middle East conflict, clouding the outlook for inflation and the rupee.
The benchmark 6.94% 2036 bond yield is likely to move in a range of 6.78% to 6.83%, a trader with a private-sector bank said.
The yield settled at 6.8012% on Wednesday.

items.title: Australian dollar gains as jobs jump, stoking rate-hike risk
items.summary:
SYDNEY: The Australian dollar hopped higher on Thursday after surprisingly strong jobs data narrowed the odds of a further hike in interest rates, intensifying the upward pressure on bond yields from surging oil prices.
The upbeat release lifted the Aussie 0.3% to $0.7016, setting up a test of the week’s top of $0.7026. Near-term support lies at around $0.6960.
The figures showed employment jumped

items.title: Global power demand to accelerate in 2026 and 2027, IEA says
items.summary:
PARIS: Global electricity demand is expected to continue to grow faster in 2026 and 2027, with renewables expected to take over as the world’s largest source of electricity generation in 2026, the International Energy Agency said in its Electricity Mid-Year Update.
Global electricity demand is expected to grow by 3.6% in 2026 and 3.8% in 2027, up from 3% in 2025, the IEA said.

items.title: Renault first-half sales volumes slip 0.4% vs year-ago period
items.summary:
PARIS: Renault reported a slight drop in sales volumes in the first half of the year on Thursday, as higher competition in its key European market from new Chinese rivals curbed growth in some of its brands.
The French automaker said it sold 1,165,133 cars and vans in the first six months of the year, down 0.4% on the same period of 2025.
In Europe, accounting for 70% of the group’s sales volumes,

items.title: Nokia Q2 profit beat on AI demand
items.summary:
STOCKHOLM: Nokia reported a bigger than expected rise in its quarterly comparable operating profit on Thursday, as the Finnish telecom gear maker got a boost from artificial intelligence and cloud customers.
Comparable operating profit jumped 18% to 434 million euros ($496.11 million) in the second quarter of 2026.
That was above the average estimate of 382 million euros from analysts polled by LS

items.title: Roche first-half sales fall on strong Swiss franc
items.summary:
Swiss drugmaker Roche said on Thursday that its first-half sales fell in Swiss franc terms, dragged down by a strong domestic currency.
‘Rare Disease Day’ marked: Roche Pakistan holds panel discussion to raise awareness
Group sales came in at 30.36 billion Swiss francs ($37.34 billion), in line with average analyst expectations of about 30.31 billion francs cited by Visible Alpha.

items.title: Rubio, Lavrov held meeting in Manila, TASS says
items.summary:
MOSCOW: Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov and US Secretary of State Marco Rubio held a meeting on the sidelines of a forum in Manila on Thursday, the TASS news agency reported.
The meeting lasted over half an hour, it added.
Lavrov and Rubio planned to discuss issues relating to the resolution of the conflict in Ukraine.

items.title: Hyundai Motor reports 21% drop in Q2 operating profit, misses forecasts
items.summary:
SEOUL: Hyundai Motor reported a 21% fall in second-quarter operating profit on Thursday, missing analysts’ estimates, as weaker vehicle sales, production disruptions and higher costs outweighed support from a weaker won.
Hyundai, which together with affiliate Kia Corp is the world’s third-biggest automaking group by sales, posted operating profit of 2.9 trillion won ($1.98 billion) for the April-J

items.title: UniCredit posts better than expected results after Commerzbank bid
items.summary:
MILAN: Italy’s second-biggest bank, UniCredit, on Thursday posted better than expected first-half earnings and slightly improved its 2026 outlook, adding it expected its investment in Commerzbank would return 15%.
UniCredit, which first invested in Commerzbank in 2024 sparking a backlash in Germany, in May launched a low-ball offer which this month handed it 48% of the German rival’s share capital

items.title: Nestle beats Q2 organic sales expectations, forms waters joint venture
items.summary:
LONDON: Nestle on Thursday posted slightly better than expected second-quarter organic sales growth and said it expects to raise around 3 billion euros ($3.43 billion) from a joint venture with investment firm Platinum Equity for its waters and premium beverages business.
The world’s biggest packaged food company also said it now expects full-year organic sales to grow 3% to 4%, instead of its pre

items.title: Govt raises Rs239.3bn in inaugural Hybrid Sukuk auction
items.summary:
The federal government on Wednesday raised Rs239.325 billion through the inaugural auction of its Short-Term Government of Pakistan (GoP) Hybrid Sukuk, “marking a new milestone in Pakistan’s Islamic capital market”.
The auction was conducted by the Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) on behalf of the Ministry of Finance (MoF), said PSX.

items.title: First Abu Dhabi Bank quarterly profit rises on higher operating income
items.summary:
First Abu Dhabi Bank, the UAE’s biggest bank by assets, reported a 4% rise in second-quarter net profit on Thursday, helped by higher operating income.
Net profit for the three months ended June 30 rose to 5.72 billion dirhams ($1.56 billion), from 5.51 billion dirhams a year earlier.
Operating income increased 7% year-on-year to 10.17 billion dirhams, while net interest income jumped 15%.

items.title: Buying observed at bourse, KSE-100 up 600 points
items.summary:
Buying interest returned at the Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX), with the benchmark KSE-100 Index gaining nearly 600 points during the opening minutes of trading on Thursday.
At 9:38am, the benchmark index was hovering at 175,023.62, up by 593.70 points or 0.34%.
Buying was observed in key sectors, including commercial banks, fertiliser, oil and gas exploration companies, OMCs, power generation and

items.title: Finance minister reviews Pakistan's macroeconomic performance in meeting with IMF officials
items.summary:
Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb held meetings with the senior officials of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) and reviewed Pakistan’s macroeconomic performance and progress under the Fund’s programme, the finance ministry said on Thursday.
In a post on social media platform X, the finance ministry said that the minister held a series of meetings with the Fund’s senior management, including

items.title: Indian billionaire Adani considering starting an airline, sources say
items.summary:
NEW DELHI: Indian billionaire Gautam Adani’s group is considering launching a new airline, a move that could potentially reshape competition in a market dominated by IndiGo and Air India, said two sources with direct knowledge of the matter.
The group is still considering its options and no final decision has been taken, the sources said, declining to be named as they were not authorised to speak

items.title: One killed, four injured in Ukrainian attack on Crimea, authorities say
items.summary:
MOSCOW: One person was killed and four injured in a Ukrainian attack on the Russian-controlled peninsula of Crimea, the Russian-installed governor said.
There are two children among the injured, Crimean governor Sergei Aksyonov wrote on Telegram.
The governor of the Ulyanovsk region east of Moscow said that Ukrainian drones had attacked an energy facility, adding it had caught fire.

items.title: Pakistan, IMF review reform progress
items.summary:
Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb on Wednesday briefed senior International Monetary Fund (IMF) officials on Pakistan’s improving fiscal and external position, as the government pushes ahead with structural reforms.

Industrial policy, out of sequence
items.summary:
Industrial policy is back in vogue, and now the very institution that for 30 years has been telling us how bad it is has turned 180 degrees.

items.title: Indian rupee to remain under pressure on runaway oil rally; Brent tops $96
items.summary:
MUMBAI: The Indian rupee is expected to remain under pressure through Thursday’s session as the worsening US-Iran dispute fuels a further ​rally in oil prices.
The Indian rupee is expected to trade in a ‌96.50-96.70 range, with risks tilted to the downside, although support from the Reserve Bank of India is likely to limit losses, a currency trader at a private ​sector bank said.

items.title: Top diplomats gather for security forum in Southeast Asia
items.summary:
MANILA: Top diplomats from Asia, Europe and North America gathered on Thursday for the security-focused ASEAN Regional Forum in Manila, ​where ministers were expected to issue calls for a peaceful settlement after renewed and intensifying hostilities in the Middle ‌East.
The war in Iran and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz could dominate discussions at the plenary event, which will be attended

items.title: Shah nominated Democratic candidate in Arizona 1st District US House race, US media projects
items.summary:
WASHINGTON: Moderate Amish Shah ​on Wednesday won ‌the Democratic Party primary election ​in Arizona’s ​US House of ⁠Representatives first ​congressional district, according ​to media projections.

items.title: Fresh violence at Delhi youth protest demanding minister's resignation
items.summary:
NEW DELHI: Youth protesters demanding the education minister’s resignation clashed briefly with police on the streets of New Delhi late on Wednesday, ‌with officers firing tear gas and wielding canes to push them back, police and witnesses said.

items.title: US-Iran tensions underpin dollar as yen nears 40-year low
items.summary:
TOKYO:  The dollar largely stabilised on Thursday as renewed US-Iran tensions kept investors on edge and underpinned demand for the safe-haven currency, ‌while the yen languished near a 40-year low with little sign of a turnaround.
The dollar index, which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies including the yen and the euro, eased 0.06% to 101.05. The greenback has gained as the wor

items.title: Oil prices rise to six-week high as US-Iran tensions escalate
items.summary:
Oil ​prices rose more than 1.5% to their highest in more than six weeks on Thursday, ‌with the United States launching a new round of strikes on Iran and Yemen’s Houthis targeting oil tankers in the Red Sea.
Brent crude futures rose $1.93, or 2% to $96 by 0011 GMT, the highest since June 8. It had settled over $3 higher at $94.07 in ​the previous session, just shy of a six-week high.

items.title: Indian shares likely to fall as oil surge dents sentiment
items.summary:
Indian equities are likely to open lower on Thursday, as oil prices climbed past $95 a barrel after ​the US launched fresh strikes on Iran and Yemen’s Houthis targeted ‌oil tankers in the Red Sea, hurting global risk appetite.
Higher oil prices pose a key risk for India, the world’s third-largest ​crude importer and consumer, by stoking inflation, widening the ​trade gap, squeezing growth and corp

items.title: Asian stocks rise after US tech earnings, oil at six-week highs
items.summary:
SINGAPORE: Asian stocks rose on Thursday after U.S. technology firms outlined significant capital spending plans that are likely to benefit chipmakers in ​the region, while the escalating war in the Middle East sent oil prices to six-week highs.
Rising oil prices have also renewed inflationary concerns, pushing ‌short-term US Treasury yields to 17-week highs as traders wager the Federal Reserve ma

items.title: Gold off two-week peak as oil advances, Fed meeting eyed
items.summary:
Gold traded below two-week highs on Thursday as an escalating Middle East conflict drove oil prices higher, while traders shifted focus to next ​week’s Federal Reserve meeting for clues on the timing of potential ‌interest rate hikes.
Spot gold was little changed at $4,133.39 per ounce as of 0115 GMT, having climbed to $4,165.87 in the last session, its highest since July 7.

items.title: Samsung invited to make Pakistan its export hub
items.summary:
ISLAMABAD: Pakistan has invited Samsung to set up the country as a regional hub for manufacturing, refurbishing, and exporting mobile phones.
The invitation was extended during a meeting between Commerce Minister Jam Kamal Khan and a delegation led by Samsung Pakistan’s Country Head at the Ministry of Commerce on Wednesday.

items.summary:
KARACHI: The federal government raised Rs768 billion through the auction of Treasury bills on Wednesday, reflecting no change in the borrowing pattern from the previous fiscal year.

The banks came out with huge liquidity, over Rs2 trillion, to invest in government papers, the easiest way to earn risk-free profits.

items.summary:
HOUSTON: Oil prices settled at their highest since June 11 on Wednesday on mounting supply concerns as hostilities continued to escalate between the US and Iran, while threats to shipping by the Iran-backed Houthi militia in Yemen further boosted prices.

items.title: Geopolitical fears drag PSX below 175,000
items.summary:
KARACHI: Amid looming energy turmoil and a deteriorating situation in the Middle East, investors grew jittery, leaving the market to bears. As a result, nervous selling on the Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) dragged the benchmark KSE-100 index below 175,000 on Wednesday.
